key_insight: PhononScore is a fast, phonon-aware scoring function that ranks crystal candidates by dynamical stability directly from structure, avoiding expensive explicit phonon calculations.
engineering_relevance: It provides a second-level stability filter for high-throughput materials discovery, making it practical for reranking, active learning, and reinforcement learning in closed-loop crystal generation.
method: Built a multi-fidelity phonon dataset with 157,463 crystal structures.
Trained a unified model to predict a dynamical-stability score from crystal structures.
Used the score to rerank outputs from crystal generation models and a DFT-finetuned variant for higher-fidelity screening.
evidence_signal: On PhononBench, PhononScore raised the average stability rate of candidate pools from 30.7% to 83.7% and achieved 97.5% stability in the Top-10; the DFT-finetuned model reached 93.0% Top-100 stability on a high-fidelity benchmark.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the coverage and fidelity of the training data, so transfer to chemically out-of-distribution crystals or truly novel compositions should be validated carefully.
paper_title: PhononScore: a phonon-aware scoring function for dynamical stability
